---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: true
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]

---

```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{Clinical Research Methodology\\ \vspace{1em} \emph{1. Introduction}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{../Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{../Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{\today}
\maketitle
```

## Today's Lecture

-   Presentation of the instructor and the course.
-   Organizational aspects of the course.
-   Learning objectives and "spirit" of the course.


## Giorgio Arcara's CV

\footnotesize

-   Degree in Psychology at Unipd (2005)

-   Master's in Neuropsychology of Acquired Cognitive Disorders (2006)

-   Various post-docs at departments (psychology, engineering, neuroscience) <br>

-   Since 2016 Researcher at IRCCS San Camillo, Venice.

-   Since 2025 Associate Professor in Psychometrics - University of Padua.

\pause
![](../Figures/GEMS.png){width="60%" fig-align="right"}

## Contacts and References
\centering

[[https://giorgioarcara.github.io]{.underline}](https://giorgioarcara.github.io)

[[unipd personal page]{.underline}](https://www.dpg.unipd.it/category/ruoli/personale-docente?key=4D0707A2DD6FCCD50B90E4C9F316A625)

[giorgio.arcara\@unipd.it](mailto:giorgio.arcara@unipd.it){.underline}


## Organizational Aspects - Materials

**I will share all the materials used and shown**

```{=latex}
The main materials are the slides and additional materials that will be provided to you during the course. You will also find the materials on the moodle page.
```


## Organizational Aspects - Materials

```{=latex}
\begin{columns}
\column{0.5\textwidth}
\small
\emph{Mondini, S., Cappelletti, M., \& Arcara, G. (2022). Methodology in Neuropsychological Assessment: An Interpretative Approach to Guide Clinical Practice. Taylor \& Francis.}\\
\vspace{1em}
In-depth text, \textbf{not essential}.

\column{0.5\textwidth}

\begin{figure}
\includegraphics[scale=0.5]{../Figures/Methodology_book.png}
\end{figure}

\end{columns}
```
## Organizational Aspects - Additional Materials

-   Free book on basic statistics and R \href{https://learningstatisticswithr.com/}{\underline{https://learningstatisticswithr.com/}}

-   Free book on psychometrics (more advanced)
\href{https://personality-project.org/r/book/}{\underline{https://personality-project.org/r/book/}}

-   Book in progress
\href{https://giorgioarcara.github.io/oip-book/}{\underline{https://giorgioarcara.github.io/oip-book/}}

## A Few Questions

_Who among you thinks you will mainly work in clinical practice?_ \pause

_Who among you thinks you will mainly work in research?_ \pause

_Who among you thinks you will use tests in clinical practice?_

## The Approach
\centering

**Learning-by-doing**

**Active-learning**

## Clinical Practice and Research
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[height=7cm]{../Figures/Separazione.png}
\end{center}


## Course Objectives

- Explain how to use tests in clinical practice \pause
- Explain the limitations of test use \pause
- Explain foundations of clinical research (for those who work clinically)

## A Step Back to Reflect

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[height=8cm]{../Figures/Stepping_back.png}
\end{center}

## Critical Use of LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT) for the Course 1/2

Large Language Models (LLMs) like ChatGPT, commonly called artificial intelligence systems, are a great opportunity for technological innovation that deserves discussion. Below are some recommendations for appropriate use in this course.


## Critical Use of LLMs (e.g., ChatGPT) for the Course 2/2

- Do not seek answers, but seek a _guide_ to find information. LLMs function as advanced search engines, especially if the question is vague.

- Use them to better understand certain concepts. If something is unclear, you can explicitly ask them to explain it in other terms or with other examples.

- **Do not blindly trust what they say** but seek confirmation from reliable sources. LLMs often show behaviors called _hallucinations_ where they give plausible or credible answers that turn out to be wrong or completely made up.
